name: Banking — Customer Success
current_tools: Cursor (emerging adoption), no centralised AI platform
pain_points: Siloed AI work with no centralised vision. No dedicated resources (side-of-desk). Deployment gap blocking productivity. Tool proliferation without standards. Executive pressure from MENA.
notes: Same stage as insurance was November 2025. Diya initiated cross-OU knowledge-sharing. Follow-up scheduled week of 23 March. Strong parallel to insurance journey — potential for shared infrastructure via App Factory.

name: Banking — Credit Team
current_tools: Cursor, MCP integrations, Copilot Studio, custom agents
pain_points: None significant — team has solved most core AI adoption challenges independently
notes: 2-3 levels ahead of insurance on AI training. Structured Agent Days framework with pre-work, transit teachers, bi-weekly follow-ups. Non-coder adoption success (market sizing in 4 hours vs 1 week). Quarterly cross-OU cadence agreed. In-person lunch at Minster Court planned.

name: Product & Technology
current_tools: CLARA (daily usage by MD), internal product tooling
pain_points: Data quality of blocker descriptions limiting thematic analysis. User Voice integration not yet enabled. Duplicate data entry with CSM teams.
notes: MD Cihan personally uses CLARA daily. Imported ~4,000-5,000 viewers via SSO. Full team (~40-50 people) attended demo 13 March. Product obsession culture, pod structure, technical sophistication. Next: per-customer AI agents, User Voice integration, feedback process.

name: Life Insurance
current_tools: None engaged yet — exploring NME and App Factory potential
pain_points: Uncertainty about IRP capabilities. Model deployment constraints (MATLAB/Python models). Cloud migration strategy questions.
notes: Early education phase. Interested in Native Modelling Engine (NME) for containerised model deployment. Christian Curran focused on security/SSO. Next step: introduction to Derek Neil (NME product owner, California) for deeper dive.

name: Sales
current_tools: Cursor, Power BI, SPM (Tableau backend), Salesforce
pain_points: Manual data ingestion workflows. Lack of API access for SPM data. Security governance barriers on Tableau/SPM data access. Commission data sensitivity.
notes: Julia Valencia (sales analytics) building independent PowerPoint generator — Slidey convergence opportunity. Has direct Salesforce CDR database access (key enabler). Dan in Vienna until late week. Next: confirm data source ownership, request API access from SPM team, deploy Dan's app to App Factory.

name: Customer & Model Success
pain_points: Tableau deprecation creating urgent demand for alternatives. Overwhelming dashboard requests from customers. Model Success team dashboarding scattered across tools.
notes: Lucia immediately saw App Factory value. Joel (R/Streamlit developer) keen to explore App Factory hosting. Maintaining Confluence documentation. First external App Factory customer. Urgent need due to Tableau sunset timeline.

name: Asset Management
notes: Referenced in Richard's strategic framing as part of cross-OU demand signal. Engagement being organised for cross-OU showcase. Not yet directly engaged.
